docs.title: TERRA UC1 - Containerized ROS 1 to ROS 2 Bridge
docs.text: This deployment is Alpha v0.0.1 of the TERRA USV Module ROS Communication Bridge for Use Case 1: assessment of water contamination in coastal areas and in the water cycle. It is a cross-platform, containerized ROS1-to-ROS2 bridge engineered for robust robotic interoperability, ensuring seamless data flow between legacy systems and modern autonomous frameworks.

docs.title: Product Chain Components
docs.text:
• USV Module ROS Communication Bridge: Implemented in Alpha v0.0.1. Bridges different versions of the Robot Operating System (ROS) to ensure seamless data flow between legacy systems and modern frameworks.
  • Autonomous Navigation Workflow Integration: Foundations only; full end-to-end autonomous navigation integration is planned for a future release.

See Component 2.XX for the scope and status of this component.

docs.title: Capabilities
docs.text:
• ROS 1 to ROS 2 Dynamic Bridging: Supports bi-directional, cross-version communication between ROS Noetic and ROS Foxy.
  • Automatic Topic Discovery: Dynamically discovers and maps topics and message types across environments at runtime.
  • Containerized Deployment: Optimized for rapid deployment and multi-architecture compatibility (x86 and ARM-based edge platforms) using Docker.
  • DDS Middleware Optimization: Employs CycloneDDS for optimized discovery, low-latency transport, and real-time topic synchronization.
  • Multi-Device Networking: Facilitates seamless multi-device networking (e.g., bridging an edge Raspberry Pi 4 to a master bridge host node).

docs.title: Current Scope
docs.text: This alpha release is a containerized, CLI-driven ROS communication bridge verified during real-world Unmanned Surface Vehicle (USV) field trials at the Sperchios River. It does not yet feature automated dynamic custom message type reconfiguration or full integration into the core autonomous navigation loop.

docs.title: Main Entry Point
docs.text: ```bash

docs.title: Build the multi-architecture image
docs.text: docker build -t ros-bridge:amd64 .

docs.title: Spin up the bridge container with host network permissions
docs.text: docker run -it --net=host --entrypoint /bin/bash ros-bridge:amd64

docs.title: Start the dynamic bridge layer
docs.text: source /ros1_bridge_ws/install/setup.bash export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://:11311 export RMW_IMPLEMENTATION=rmw_cyclonedds_cpp ros2 run ros1_bridge dynamic_bridge

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
The ros1-to-ros2-bridge deployment provides interoperability between ROS 1 and ROS 2 ecosystems using the ros1_bridge dynamic bridge.

The deployment is containerized and enables bidirectional communication between ROS Noetic and ROS 2 Foxy nodes without requiring modifications to existing applications.

docs.title: Component Boundaries
docs.text: Component Implementation ROS 1 Runtime ROS Noetic ROS 2 Runtime ROS 2 Foxy Bridge Layer ros1_bridge dynamic bridge

docs.title: Runtime Flow
docs.text:
1. A ROS 1 master is started.
  2. ROS 1 publishers and subscribers register with the master.
  3. ROS 2 DDS discovery becomes available.
  4. The dynamic bridge inspects both middleware environments.
  5. Matching message definitions are detected automatically.
  6. Bidirectional forwarding routes are created.
  7. Messages flow transparently between ROS 1 and ROS 2.

docs.title: Middleware
docs.text:
The deployment uses CycloneDDS:

export RMW_IMPLEMENTATION=rmw_cyclonedds_cpp

CycloneDDS provides reliable DDS discovery and communication across distributed deployments.

docs.title: Container Contents
docs.text:
The published image contains:

  • ROS Noetic
  • ROS 2 Foxy
  • ros1_bridge
  • CycloneDDS
  • Runtime startup scripts

docs.title: External Dependencies
docs.text:
The deployment requires:

  • Docker Engine
  • Network connectivity between hosts
  • ROS Master availability
  • DDS multicast support or equivalent network routing

docs.title: Bridge Operation
docs.text: This document describes the operational procedure for establishing communication between ROS 1 and ROS 2 systems.

docs.title: Start the ROS Master
docs.text:
On the ROS 1 host:

export ROS_IP=<ros1_container_IP>
export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://<ros1_container_IP>:11311

roscore

Expected output:

started roslaunch server
ROS_MASTER_URI=http://<ros1_container_IP>:11311

docs.title: Start the Bridge Container
docs.text:
docker start -ai <ros_bridge_container_id>

Configure the environment:

source /ros1_bridge_ws/install/setup.bash

export ROS_IP=<ros2_container_IP>
export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://<ros1_container_IP>:11311
export RMW_IMPLEMENTATION=rmw_cyclonedds_cpp

Start the bridge:

ros2 run ros1_bridge dynamic_bridge --bridge-all-topics

Expected output:

created 1to2 bridge for topic '/rosout_agg'
created 2to1 bridge for topic '/rosout'

docs.title: Verify ROS 2 Discovery
docs.text:
Open a new terminal:

ros2 topic list

Example:

/parameter_events
/rosout
/rosout_agg

docs.title: Stop the Bridge
docs.text:
Press:

CTRL+C

or stop the container:

docker stop <ros_bridge_container_id>

docs.title: Networking
docs.text: The bridge requires direct network connectivity between ROS 1 and ROS 2 environments.

docs.title: ROS 1 Configuration
docs.text:
export ROS_IP=192.168.XX.XXX
export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://192.168.XX.XXX:11311

docs.title: ROS 2 Configuration
docs.text: export RMW_IMPLEMENTATION=rmw_cyclonedds_cpp

docs.title: Connectivity Tests
docs.text:
Verify network reachability:

ping 192.168.XX.XXX

Verify ROS Master accessibility:

curl http://192.168.XX.XXX:11311

Verify topic visibility:

rostopic list

docs.title: Common Networking Issues
docs.text:
• Incorrect ROS_IP values
  • Multiple active interfaces
  • Firewall restrictions
  • Docker bridge isolation
  • DDS discovery blocked by network policies

docs.title: Supported Platforms
docs.text: Platform Status Ubuntu 20.04 Supported Docker Engine 24+ Supported Raspberry Pi 4 & 5 (64-bit) Supported x86_64 Linux Supported

docs.title: Minimum Requirements
docs.text: Resource Requirement CPU 2 cores RAM 4 GB Disk 5 GB Network Layer 2 or Layer 3 connectivity

docs.title: Docker Installation Verification
docs.text:
docker --version
docker compose version

docs.title: Pulling the Image
docs.text: docker push losandriana/ros1-to-ros2-bridge:tagname

docs.title: Network Requirements
docs.text:
The following services must be reachable:

Service Port ROS Master 11311/TCP DDS Discovery Dynamic UDP Docker Networks Internal

docs.title: Verification
docs.text:
Verify connectivity to the ROS master:

ping <ros1_container_IP>
curl http://<ros1_container_IP>:11311

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
This page demonstrates a complete ROS 1 to ROS 2 bridge deployment using the provided Docker containers and networking configuration.

The example environment consists of:

Component Address Raspberry Pi 4 (ROS 1 Master) Bridge Host Middleware CycloneDDS ROS Versions Noetic ↔ Foxy

docs.title: 1 - Start the ROS 1 Master
docs.text:
Start the ROS 1 container on the Raspberry Pi:

docker start -ai <ros1_container_id>

Configure ROS networking:

export ROS_IP=<ros1_container_IP>
export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://<ros1_container_IP>:11311

Start the ROS master:

roscore

Expected output:

started roslaunch server
ROS_MASTER_URI=http://<ros1_container_IP>:11311/
process[master]: started
process[rosout-1]: started

The ROS master is now running and accepting ROS 1 node registrations.

docs.title: Screenshot
docs.text: The screenshot shows a ROS Noetic master successfully running on the Raspberry Pi 4.

docs.title: 2 - Start the ROS 1 to ROS 2 Bridge
docs.text:
Start the bridge container:

docker start -ai <ros_bridge_container_id>

Configure the runtime environment:

source /ros1_bridge_ws/install/setup.bash

export ROS_IP=<ros2_container_IP>
export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://<ros1_container_IP>:11311
export RMW_IMPLEMENTATION=rmw_cyclonedds_cpp

Launch the dynamic bridge:

ros2 run ros1_bridge dynamic_bridge

Expected output:

created 1to2 bridge for topic '/rosout_agg'
created 2to1 bridge for topic '/rosout'

The bridge automatically discovers compatible message types and creates forwarding routes.

docs.title: Screenshot
docs.text: The bridge is actively connected to the ROS 1 master and has established topic translation routes.

docs.title: 3 - Verify ROS 2 Topic Discovery
docs.text:
Open a new terminal on the bridge host and inspect ROS 2 topics:

ros2 topic list

Example output:

/parameter_events
/rosout
/rosout_agg

The bridged ROS 1 topics should now appear inside the ROS 2 environment.

docs.title: Screenshot
docs.text: The screenshot demonstrates successful DDS discovery and ROS 2 topic visibility through the bridge.

docs.title: 4 - Verify ROS 2 Runtime
docs.text:
Inspect the ROS 2 environment:

ros2 node list

Inspect topic information:

ros2 topic info /rosout_agg

Inspect active publishers:

ros2 topic echo /rosout_agg

docs.title: Screenshot
docs.text: This terminal shows the ROS 2 environment successfully interacting with topics exposed through the bridge.

docs.title: 5 - End-to-End Message Verification
docs.text:
Publish a ROS 1 message:

rostopic pub /test_topic std_msgs/String "data: 'hello from ros1'"

From ROS 2:

ros2 topic echo /test_topic

Expected output:

data: hello from ros1

Publish from ROS 2:

ros2 topic pub /test_topic std_msgs/msg/String "{data: 'hello from ros2'}"

From ROS 1:

rostopic echo /test_topic

Expected output:

data: hello from ros2

Successful message exchange confirms bidirectional communication between ROS 1 and ROS 2.

docs.title: Validation Checklist
docs.text:
After completing the examples:

  • [ ] ROS Master is running
  • [ ] ROS Master is reachable from the bridge host
  • [ ] Dynamic bridge started successfully
  • [ ] ROS 2 topics are visible
  • [ ] DDS discovery is operational
  • [ ] Topic routes were created automatically
  • [ ] ROS 1 → ROS 2 communication works
  • [ ] ROS 2 → ROS 1 communication works

When all checklist items are satisfied, the bridge deployment is operating correctly.

docs.title: Bridge Starts But No Topics Appear
docs.text:
Check ROS 2 discovery:

ros2 topic list

Verify ROS Master:

echo $ROS_MASTER_URI

Expected:

http://<ros_bridge_container_IP>:11311

docs.title: No Bridge Routes Created
docs.text:
Check ROS 1 topic types:

rostopic type /topic_name

Check ROS 2 topic types:

ros2 topic type /topic_name

The bridge only creates routes for matching message definitions.

docs.title: Multiple Network Interfaces
docs.text:
Warning:

using network interface wlan0 selected arbitrarily

Specify the desired interface:

export ROS_IP=<ros2_container_IP>

docs.title: DDS Discovery Problems
docs.text:
Verify middleware:

echo $RMW_IMPLEMENTATION

Expected:

rmw_cyclonedds_cpp

docs.title: Container Diagnostics
docs.text:
Inspect logs:

docker logs <ros_bridge_container_id>

Open a shell:

docker exec -it <ros_bridge_container_id> bash

docs.title: Verify ROS Master Reachability
docs.text:
ping <ros_bridge_container_IP>
curl http://<ros_bridge_container_IP>:11311
